source.title: From Spontaneous Ignitions to Sensorimotor Cell Assemblies via Dopamine: A Spiking Neurocomputational Model of Infants' Hand Action Acquisition

confidence_rationale: Neurobiologically constrained spiking neural network model (six brain area maps; excitatory/inhibitory; STDP + Hebbian + dopamine-modulated reward). Two-phase model -- Hebbian self-organization first, dopamine-reward consolidation second -- directly formalizes the babbling-to-action-consolidation pipeline. Source quality moderate (Brain Sciences is broad; model is simulation only). Mapping fidelity strong because the two-phase architecture (exploration then reward-consolidation) is exactly what REE needs for its infant-stage substrate. A pure reward-from-birth architecture would skip phase 1 and produce no option diversity.
failure_signatures: Simulation only; no direct comparison with infant neurophysiological data on cell assembly formation.
Dopamine is used as a global reward signal -- this may not capture the context-specific neuromodulation required for flexible repertoire formation.
The model is tested only on hand action acquisition; generalization to whole-body or locomotor repertoires is unverified.

mapping.source_claim_statement: During an initial Hebbian exploratory phase, the neural architecture develops action-perception circuits for multiple hand actions via spontaneous motor babbling (noise-driven ignitions). In a second phase, dopamine-mediated reward reinforces a subset of those circuits, increasing their spontaneous ignition frequency and driving the transition from exploration to reward-seeking.
mapping.ree_translation: The two-phase model maps precisely onto REE's infant-stage design problem. Phase 1 (Hebbian self-organization) is what the infant substrate must support without yet having a calibrated reward signal. Phase 2 (dopamine consolidation) is what the agent does once the harm/goal signals are available. Without Phase 1, the agent has no option diversity for Phase 2 to select from. This is the mechanistic justification for a dedicated exploration phase before reward-based learning.
mapping.mapping_caveat: REE uses CEM + E3 scoring rather than spiking neural nets; the Hebbian + dopamine mechanism is one implementation of the two-phase logic, not a prescription for the REE architecture.
mapping.source_context: Computational simulation; 6-area model covering motor cortex, premotor cortex, somatosensory regions; simulating human hand action development.

confidence_components.notes: High conceptual alignment with REE infant-stage problem; moderate source quality (simulation, broad-scope journal).
